title: Measuring the Impact of Early-2025 AI on Experienced Open-Source Developer Productivity
notes: Closest methodological match: RCT with 16 developers, task-level randomization, real coding work, real outcomes. Missing: no memory system evaluated, N=16 not n=1, no pre-registration mentioned.

title: Developer Productivity With and Without GitHub Copilot: A Longitudinal Mixed-Methods Case Study
notes: Longitudinal 2-year study of real developers with AI coding assistant. Missing: no memory system, N=39, no pre-registered protocol, no randomized memory configurations, no time-series task-level outcomes.

title: The First Controlled Benchmark of AI Memory in Coding Agents (Stompy/Sandelin)
notes: CLOSEST STRUCTURAL MATCH. Single codebase, controlled memory conditions (on/off/hybrid), real coding tasks, outcome metrics (quality, tokens, turns). BUT: only 9 runs (not >50 sessions), not pre-registered, not longitudinal, blog post not peer-reviewed, explicitly a pilot. Author explicitly plans 27-session longitudinal follow-up NOT YET PUBLISHED.

title: Evolving with AI: A Longitudinal Analysis of Developer Logs
notes: 800 developers, 2-year telemetry, AI coding assistants. Longitudinal but not memory system, not n=1, not pre-registered, no randomized conditions.

title: LoCoMo: Evaluating Very Long-Term Conversational Memory of LLM Agents (ACL 2024)
notes: Multi-session (up to 35 sessions, 300 turns) memory benchmark. Synthetic dialogues, not real developer deployment, not coding-specific, not pre-registered.

title: Hindsight is 20/20: Building Agent Memory that Retains, Recalls, and Reflects
notes: Technical agent memory paper, benchmark evaluation (LongMemEval, LoCoMo). Not real-user deployment, not longitudinal field study, not n=1.

title: SWE-Bench-CL: Continual Learning for Coding Agents
notes: Chronological coding task sequences for continual learning evaluation, FAISS memory. Synthetic benchmark, no human developer in loop, not pre-registered.

title: From Tools to Teammates: Evaluating LLMs in Multi-Session Coding Interactions (ACL 2025)
notes: Multi-session coding memory evaluation. Synthetic MemoryCode dataset. No real developer deployment.

title: Enabling Personalized Long-term Interactions in LLM-based Agents through Persistent Memory and User Profiles
notes: Agent memory system with a 5-day pilot user study. Real users, real memory system, perceived personalization outcomes. BUT: only 5 days, not coding-specific, not >50 sessions, no pre-registration, participant count not disclosed.

title: LongMemEval-V2: Evaluating Long-Term Agent Memory Toward Experienced Colleagues
notes: Agent memory benchmark with coding variant (AgentRunbook-C). 451 curated questions. Synthetic, not real-user deployment, not longitudinal field study.

title: We are Changing our Developer Productivity Experiment Design — METR
notes: METR retrospective acknowledging measurement failures of longitudinal developer productivity studies. Useful as evidence that rigorous within-person AI coding study methodology remains unsolved. No memory system.

title: State of AI Agent Memory 2026: Benchmarks, Architectures & Production Gaps
notes: Industry survey explicitly identifying lack of real-world user studies as a production gap for agent memory systems. Supports novelty claim.

title: Examining the Use and Impact of an AI Code Assistant on Developer Productivity and Experience in the Enterprise (CHI EA '25)
notes: 669 survey respondents + usability study on IBM watsonx Code Assistant. Real enterprise deployment but no memory system, not longitudinal, not n=1.

title: OpenAgents: An Open Platform for Language Agents in the Wild
notes: STALE-RISK. Real users interact with agents in the wild (Data Agent, Plugins, Web). Not longitudinal, not memory-system evaluation, not n=1.

title: Intuition to Evidence: Measuring AI's True Impact on Developer Productivity
notes: 300 engineers, 12-month longitudinal study, within-subjects controls. Real coding work, real productivity outcomes. Missing: no memory system, N=300, not n=1, not pre-registered.

title: MemoryArena: Benchmarking Agent Memory in Interdependent Multi-Session Agentic Tasks
notes: Multi-session memory benchmark for agents. Synthetic agentic tasks. No real human developer deployment.

title: How Memory Management Impacts LLM Agents: An Empirical Study of Experience-Following Behavior
notes: Laboratory empirical study of memory management in LLM agents. No real developer deployment, no longitudinal design.

title: Letta Code: A Memory-First Coding Agent
notes: Product announcement blog. No user study, no longitudinal data, benchmark (Terminal-Bench) only.

title: Calculating Cognitive Augmentation, A Case Study
notes: STALE-RISK. Proposes formal metrics for AI cognitive augmentation via case study. Not LLM agent memory, not coding-specific, not longitudinal.

title: AI IDEs or Autonomous Agents? Measuring the Impact of Coding Agents on Software Development
notes: Calls for longitudinal evidence on autonomous coding agents. Itself not a longitudinal study of memory systems. No real developer deployment of memory system.
